notes: I came in expecting motivational poster content about 'just commit to the plan.' What I got is a sharper claim: branching past the point where evidence should have collapsed it is a state of intelligence, not a strategy. That is a real diagnostic I can use in a staff review. The two-engineer scenario is recognisable to anyone who has sat through architectural review — option A through G with comparison tables, no owners, 'preserve optionality' — and the verse names what is actually wrong (the prior grounding work was skipped) rather than blaming the engineer. The falsifiability handles the cargo-cult-conviction failure mode I would have raised. The counter-example correctly carves out genuine discovery phase. The verse-line 'branching past where evidence should collapse it is the diagnostic of irresolution — not of richer thinking, but of thinner grounding' is the kind of thing I would write in a PR review. PASS.
